messages.content:
user just typed "get me" - this is an incomplete/abbreviated message that doesn't make sense as a complete request. I should not assume what they meant. I need to ask them to clarify what they want.

Looking at the context, there's no indication of what "get me" refers to. It could be:
- Get me a file
- Get me information about something
- Get me X (incomplete)

The best response is to ask the user to complete their request, since "get me" alone is too vague to act on. I shouldn't guess or fabricate intent.

Per the CLAUDE.md rules:
- 不知道就说不知道，不编、不猜、不推测
- 结论优先 + 禁止无信息下的回答

I'll respond briefly asking what they need, in Chinese (per language preference).</think>

老板，"get me" 这条消息我没看到具体要什么——是文件、信息、命令还是别的什么？

请补全需求：「get me <具体内容>」。
